Title: Product Variations - Size Selection
Preconditions: Product with size variations (S/M/L/XL)
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with size variations (e.g., T-Shirt)
2. Verify size selector displays
3. Verify all sizes listed (S, M, L, XL)
4. Click size 'M'
5. Verify size selected (highlighted)
6. Verify Add to Cart enabled
7. Click Add to Cart
8. View cart
9. Verify selected size shown in cart
Expected Result:
- Size selector displays (dropdown or buttons)
- All available sizes listed
- Clicking size selects it (visual feedback)
- Only one size selectable at a time
- Add to Cart enabled after selection
- Cart displays: Product Name - Size: M
- Correct SKU for selected size variant
- Price correct for size (if size affects price)

Title: Product Variations - Color Selection
Preconditions: Product with color variations
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with colors (e.g., Blue, Red, Black, White)
2. Verify color selector displays
3. Click color 'Blue'
4. Verify color selected
5. Verify product image updates to blue variant
6. Click Add to Cart
7. View cart
Expected Result:
- Color selector displays (swatches or dropdown)
- Color swatches show actual colors
- Clicking selects color (border/checkmark)
- Main product image updates to show selected color
- Cart shows: Product Name - Color: Blue
- Correct color variant SKU

Title: Product Variations - Multi-Attribute (Size + Color)
Preconditions: Product with size and color variations
Steps:
1. Navigate to configurable product
2. Verify both size and color selectors display
3. Select Size: M
4. Verify color options update (only colors available in M)
5. Select Color: Blue
6. Verify price updates (if variant pricing)
7. Verify stock status shows
8. Click Add to Cart
9. View cart
Expected Result:
- Both selectors visible
- Selecting first attribute filters second (only valid combinations)
- Variant-specific pricing displays
- Stock status per variant: In Stock / Low Stock / Out of Stock
- Add to Cart disabled until all required attributes selected
- Cart shows: Product - Size: M, Color: Blue
- Correct variant SKU and price

Title: Product Variations - Out of Stock Variant
Preconditions: Product with some variants out of stock
Steps:
1. Navigate to configurable product
2. Select size that is out of stock
3. Verify out of stock message
4. Verify Add to Cart disabled
5. Select different size (in stock)
6. Verify Add to Cart enabled
Expected Result:
- Out of stock variants visible but disabled/grayed
- Clicking out of stock variant shows message: 'This variant is out of stock'
- Add to Cart button disabled or shows 'Out of Stock'
- Selecting in-stock variant enables Add to Cart
- Stock status badge visible per variant

Title: Product Variations - Price Updates by Variant
Preconditions: Product with variant-specific pricing (e.g., size affects price)
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with size-based pricing
2. Note default price (e.g., $29.99 for size S)
3. Select size L
4. Verify price updates (e.g., $34.99 for size L)
5. Select size XL
6. Verify price updates again (e.g., $39.99)
7. Add to cart
8. Verify cart price matches variant price
Expected Result:
- Default price displays for default/first variant
- Selecting different variant updates price immediately
- Price change animates or highlights briefly
- Old price may show strikethrough if on sale
- Cart line price matches selected variant price
- Checkout total uses correct variant pricing

- Selectors large enough (44x44px minimum)

- User can start configuration over

Title: Product Configuration - Mobile Configuration UI
Preconditions: Mobile device, configurable product
Steps:
1. Open product on mobile
2. Scroll to configuration section
3. Interact with option selectors
4. Verify mobile-optimized UI
5. Select multiple options
6. Verify price updates visible
7. Add to cart from mobile
Expected Result:
- Configuration UI responsive
- Dropdowns use native pickers on mobile
- Visual selectors (color swatches) large enough to tap
- Price updates visible (sticky footer or always visible)
- Multi-step config uses wizard/stepper (optional)
- No horizontal scrolling
- Add to Cart button accessible
- Configuration summary collapsible on mobile

Title: Product Configuration - Accessibility
Preconditions: Configurable product, keyboard/screen reader
Steps:
1. Navigate to product using keyboard only
2. Tab through configuration options
3. Verify focus indicators visible
4. Select options using keyboard (Enter/Space)
5. Enable screen reader
6. Verify options announced
7. Verify price updates announced
8. Complete configuration and add to cart
Expected Result:
- All configuration options keyboard accessible
- Tab order logical
- Focus indicators visible
- Can select options via keyboard (Enter, Space, Arrow keys)
- Screen reader announces:
  - Option labels
  - Current selection
  - Price changes
  - Required vs optional
- ARIA labels present
- Error messages announced
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliant

Title: Configurable Product - Discount Scales Correctly with Quantity
Preconditions: Configurable product with discounted options, quantity > 1
Steps:
1. Navigate to configurable product page (e.g. bike builder)
2. Select a configuration option that has a discount (list price > sale price)
3. Set quantity to 2 or more
4. Observe the subtotal in the configuration section
5. Verify in cart: subtotal = salePrice x quantity (not listPrice x quantity)
6. Verify cart discount amount = (listPrice - salePrice) x quantity
Expected Result:
- Sale price is used for subtotal calculation (not list price)
- Discount amount is multiplied by quantity: discountAmount * qty = correct total discount
- Cart subtotal is mathematically correct: salePrice x qty
- No pricing discrepancy between product page and cart
- VCST-4586 regression case

Title: Configurable Product - Sale Price Displayed as Primary Price
Preconditions: Configurable product with discounted options (list price != sale price)
Steps:
1. Navigate to configurable product
2. Select an option with a discount applied
3. Observe the price display in the configuration section
4. Verify which price is shown prominently
Expected Result:
- Sale price (salePrice) is displayed prominently (bold, dark colour)
- List price (listPrice) is shown with strikethrough formatting
- Visual presentation: salePrice = primary, listPrice = secondary/crossed out
- Price math is clear (e.g. $88.00 sale with $126.00 crossed out)

Title: Configurable Product - Mobile Sale Price Display
Preconditions: Mobile viewport (375x667), configurable product with discounted options
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 375x667
2. Navigate to configurable product
3. Select an option with a discount
4. Observe price display in configuration section on mobile
Expected Result:
- Mobile fallback price component shows sale price (not list price)
- Sale price visible in mobile configuration section
- Regression check: list price should NOT be shown as the primary price on mobile
- Testing fix for mobile bug found in VCST-4586

Title: Configurable Product - No Auto-Scroll on First Collapsed Section Click
Preconditions: Fresh page load of configurable product with multiple accordion sections (some collapsed by default)
Steps:
1. Navigate to configurable product page (fresh load - no cached state)
2. Wait for page to fully load
3. Locate a configuration accordion section that is COLLAPSED by default
4. Click the accordion header to expand it
5. Immediately click a radio button option inside the newly expanded section
6. Verify page scroll position
Expected Result:
- Page does NOT auto-scroll after the first radio button click
- Radio button selection STICKS (does not revert to 'None' or default)
- Scroll position remains where user was before click
- Selected option is confirmed as checked
- VCST-4612 regression case

Title: Configurable Product - First Click Radio Selection Sticks (No Revert)
Preconditions: Fresh page load, configurable product with collapsed accordion sections
Steps:
1. Fresh page load of configurable product
2. Expand a collapsed accordion section
3. Click a radio button option (first option in the newly expanded section)
4. Observe the radio button state after 1-2 seconds
5. Verify the selection has not reverted
Expected Result:
- Radio button remains selected after first click
- The selection does NOT revert to 'None' or the default value
- Price updates to reflect the selected option
- Section header updates to show selected option name
- VCST-4612 regression case (selection revert bug)

Title: VcVariantPickerGroup - All Variant Options Render Correctly
Preconditions: Product with multiple variation options (size, colour), Storybook changes deployed
Steps:
1. Navigate to product with size and colour variants
2. Observe the variant picker UI (colour swatches, size buttons)
3. Click various options
4. Verify visual feedback for selected, unselected, and disabled states
Expected Result:
- All variant options render (no blank spaces or broken swatches)
- Selected state visually distinct (border, checkmark, fill)
- Unselected state clearly different from selected
- Disabled/out-of-stock variant shown differently (grayed)
- No visual regression from Storybook StoryFn to StoryObj migration

Title: Org Switcher Visible for Multi-Org User
Preconditions: User belongs to 2 or more organizations, logged in to storefront
Steps:
1. Log in as a user who belongs to multiple organizations
2. Navigate to the storefront header or account area
3. Locate the organization switcher control
4. Verify it is visible and shows the current organization name
Expected Result:
- Org switcher control is visible in the header or account section
- Current organization name is displayed on the switcher
- Switcher appears only for users belonging to 2 or more organizations
- Single-org users do not see the switcher (see B2C-ORG-010)

Title: Switch Org - Pricing Update
Preconditions: Multi-org user, Org A and Org B have different contract pricing for the same product
Steps:
1. Log in and confirm Org A is active (check org switcher)
2. Navigate to a product with org-specific contract pricing
3. Note the price shown for Org A
4. Open the org switcher and select Org B
5. Navigate to the same product
6. Verify the price has updated to Org B's contract price
Expected Result:
- After switching to Org B, the product price reflects Org B's contract/tier pricing
- Price is different from Org A's price (confirming org-specific pricing is applied)
- Pricing update occurs without requiring a page reload or re-login
- Cart prices also update to reflect the new org's pricing

Title: Switch Org - Cart Isolation
Preconditions: Multi-org user, logged in as Org A
Steps:
1. Log in and confirm Org A is active
2. Add 2 specific products to the cart under Org A
3. Note the Org A cart items
4. Open the org switcher and switch to Org B
5. Navigate to the cart
6. Verify Org A cart items are not present
7. Add a different product to the Org B cart
8. Switch back to Org A
9. Verify the Org A cart still has the original 2 products
Expected Result:
- Org A cart is isolated from Org B cart — switching orgs loads that org's specific cart
- Org B cart starts empty (or shows Org B's previous cart) — Org A items are not visible
- After adding items to Org B cart and switching back, Org A cart items are preserved
- Cart count in header reflects the active org's cart

Title: Switch Org - Addresses Update
Preconditions: Multi-org user, Org A and Org B have different saved addresses
Steps:
1. Log in as Org A
2. Navigate to account addresses or the ship-to selector
3. Note the addresses available for Org A
4. Switch to Org B using the org switcher
5. Navigate to account addresses or ship-to selector again
6. Verify addresses shown belong to Org B
Expected Result:
- Addresses displayed after switching to Org B are Org B's addresses
- Org A addresses are not shown while Org B is active
- Ship-to selector in header reflects Org B's addresses
- Checkout address options reflect the active org's addresses

Title: Switch Org - Lists Scope
Preconditions: Multi-org user, Org A and Org B each have at least one saved list
Steps:
1. Log in as Org A
2. Navigate to My Lists
3. Note the lists available for Org A
4. Switch to Org B
5. Navigate to My Lists
6. Verify only Org B's lists are shown
Expected Result:
- After switching to Org B, only lists associated with Org B are displayed
- Org A lists are not visible while Org B is active
- Shared lists within an org are scoped to that org only
- Switching back to Org A restores Org A's lists

Title: Default Org on Sign-in
Preconditions: Multi-org user, one org is configured as the default
Steps:
1. Log in to the storefront with the multi-org user credentials
2. Observe which organization is loaded immediately after sign-in
3. Verify the org switcher shows the default org as active
4. Verify the default org's pricing and catalog are shown
Expected Result:
- Default org is loaded automatically on sign-in (not a random or last-used org)
- Org switcher displays the default org name as the active selection
- Default org's catalog pricing, addresses, and cart are shown
- No prompt to select an org is required on sign-in (default is auto-applied)

Title: Search Orgs in Switcher
Preconditions: Multi-org user belonging to 6 or more organizations
Steps:
1. Log in as a user with many org memberships (6+)
2. Open the org switcher
3. Locate the search input within the switcher dropdown
4. Type part of an org name (e.g., first 3 characters)
5. Verify the results filter to matching org names
6. Clear the search and verify all orgs are shown again
Expected Result:
- Search/filter input is present inside the org switcher dropdown
- Typing filters the org list dynamically to matching org names
- Non-matching orgs are hidden while search is active
- Clearing the search restores the full list of orgs
- Selecting a filtered result correctly switches to that org

Title: Switch Org - Navigation Reset
Preconditions: Multi-org user, currently on a deep category or account page for Org A
Steps:
1. Log in as Org A
2. Navigate to a deep category page or checkout flow
3. Note the breadcrumb trail
4. Switch to Org B using the org switcher
5. Verify the page state and navigation after the switch
Expected Result:
- After switching orgs, the user is redirected to a neutral page (e.g., homepage or catalog root) rather than remaining on a context-specific page for the previous org
- Breadcrumbs and category context reset to reflect Org B
- No stale Org A navigation state remains visible
- Any in-progress flows (like checkout) are exited cleanly

Title: Switch Back - Cart Intact
Preconditions: Multi-org user, items in Org A cart, currently viewing Org B
Steps:
1. Log in as Org A and add 3 specific products to the Org A cart
2. Switch to Org B
3. Optionally add a different item to the Org B cart
4. Switch back to Org A
5. Navigate to the cart
6. Verify all 3 original Org A items are present with correct quantities
Expected Result:
- Switching away from Org A and back preserves the Org A cart intact
- All originally added items are still present after returning to Org A
- Quantities and selected variants are unchanged
- Org B items are not present in Org A cart after switching back

Title: Single Org User - No Switcher
Preconditions: User belongs to exactly one organization, logged in to storefront
Steps:
1. Log in as a user who belongs to only one organization
2. Navigate to the header and account area
3. Look for the org switcher control
Expected Result:
- No org switcher control is visible in the header or account area
- The single org is active by default
- UI is not cluttered with a switcher that has no utility
- User experience is unchanged from pre-multi-org behavior for single-org users

Title: Quick Order by SKU
Preconditions: User logged in, /bulk-order page accessible, at least one known product SKU
Steps:
1. Navigate to /bulk-order (Quick Order page)
2. Locate the SKU entry field
3. Enter a known valid product SKU
4. Confirm the entry (press Enter or click Add)
5. Verify product info appears: product name, image, unit price, available quantity
Expected Result:
- Quick Order page loads at /bulk-order
- SKU entry field is present and focused
- Entering a valid SKU and confirming loads the product details in the row
- Product name, price, and availability are displayed for the entered SKU
- Quantity field becomes editable after product is found

Title: Add All to Cart
Preconditions: Quick Order page with 3+ valid SKUs entered and quantities set
Steps:
1. Navigate to /bulk-order
2. Enter 3 different valid SKUs and set quantities (e.g., 2, 5, 1)
3. Verify all 3 rows show correct product info
4. Click 'Add All to Cart'
5. Navigate to cart
6. Verify all 3 products are in the cart with correct quantities
Expected Result:
- 'Add All to Cart' button is enabled when at least one valid product row is populated
- Clicking adds all listed products to the cart simultaneously
- Cart contains all 3 products with the specified quantities
- Cart count updates to reflect all added items
- Success notification: 'X items added to cart'

Title: Invalid SKU Handling
Preconditions: Quick Order page open
Steps:
1. Navigate to /bulk-order
2. Enter a valid SKU in row 1 and confirm
3. Enter a non-existent SKU (e.g., 'FAKE-SKU-99999') in row 2 and confirm
4. Verify error state on row 2
5. Verify row 1 is unaffected
Expected Result:
- Row 2 shows an error: 'Product not found' or 'Invalid SKU'
- Row 1 with the valid SKU remains displayed correctly
- Error is scoped to the invalid row only (other rows unaffected)
- 'Add All to Cart' only processes valid rows; invalid rows are skipped or cause a specific warning

Title: Multi-row Entry
Preconditions: Quick Order page open
Steps:
1. Navigate to /bulk-order
2. Enter 5 different valid SKUs in separate rows
3. Set different quantities for each (e.g., 1, 3, 2, 5, 10)
4. Verify all 5 rows display product info correctly
5. Verify a running total or summary is calculated
Expected Result:
- All 5 rows display correctly with product name, price, and set quantity
- Page does not slow down or lose state with 5+ rows
- Running total (if displayed) sums the subtotal for all rows correctly
- Can continue adding more rows beyond 5 (or up to any stated limit)

Title: Paste from Spreadsheet
Preconditions: Quick Order page open, clipboard contains tab-separated SKU and quantity data
Steps:
1. In a text editor or spreadsheet, create 3 rows with format: SKU[tab]Quantity
2. Copy these 3 rows to clipboard
3. Navigate to /bulk-order
4. Paste (Ctrl+V) into the bulk order entry area or first SKU field
5. Verify rows are auto-populated with the pasted SKUs and quantities
Expected Result:
- Paste action auto-populates multiple rows from tab-separated clipboard data
- SKU and quantity from each pasted row are correctly assigned to separate columns
- Product lookup is triggered for each pasted SKU
- Invalid SKUs in the pasted data are flagged per row
- Valid products display correctly after paste

Title: Bulk Order Quantity Validation
Preconditions: Quick Order page with a valid product SKU entered
Steps:
1. Enter a valid SKU and confirm the product is found
2. Set the quantity field to 0
3. Attempt to add to cart
4. Verify validation error
5. Set the quantity to -1 (negative)
6. Verify validation error
7. Set a valid quantity (e.g., 1)
8. Verify add to cart succeeds
Expected Result:
- Quantity of 0 triggers validation error: 'Quantity must be at least 1'
- Negative quantity triggers validation error
- 'Add to Cart' or 'Add All to Cart' is blocked for rows with invalid quantities
- Setting a positive valid quantity clears the error and enables the add action

Title: Create Shared List
Preconditions: Logged in user who is a member of a B2B organization, on My Lists page
Steps:
1. Navigate to My Lists (/account/lists)
2. Click 'Create New List'
3. Enter list name: 'Team Supplies'
4. Set privacy/visibility to 'Shared' (visible to org members)
5. Click 'Create'
6. Verify list created successfully
7. Verify the list is marked as 'Shared'
Expected Result:
- Create list form includes a visibility option: Private vs Shared
- Selecting 'Shared' marks the list as visible to all members of the current organization
- List created successfully with 'Shared' badge or indicator
- List appears in My Lists with Shared status
- Other org members can see this list (see B2C-LIST-017)

Title: Org Member Sees Shared List
Preconditions: Shared list 'Team Supplies' created by User A, User B is a member of the same organization
Steps:
1. Log in as User B (a different org member from User A)
2. Navigate to My Lists (/account/lists)
3. Look for the list 'Team Supplies' created by User A
Expected Result:
- 'Team Supplies' list is visible to User B in the lists section
- Shared lists from other org members are displayed (e.g., in a 'Shared with me' or 'Team Lists' section)
- List shows who created it (User A's name)
- User B can view the contents of the shared list

Title: Add to Shared List
Preconditions: Shared list 'Team Supplies' visible to User B (org member)
Steps:
1. Log in as User B
2. Navigate to the shared list 'Team Supplies'
3. Click 'Add Product' or navigate to a product and add it to 'Team Supplies'
4. Verify product is added to the shared list
5. Log in as User A (list creator)
6. Navigate to 'Team Supplies'
7. Verify the product added by User B is visible
Expected Result:
- User B can add products to a shared list owned by User A
- Product added by User B appears immediately in the shared list
- When User A views the list, the item added by User B is present
- Added item shows who added it (if attribution is supported)

Title: Private List Not Visible to Org Members
Preconditions: User A has created a Private list, User B is a member of the same org
Steps:
1. Verify User A has a list set to 'Private'
2. Log in as User B (different org member)
3. Navigate to My Lists
4. Verify User A's private list is NOT visible to User B
Expected Result:
- User A's Private list does not appear in User B's My Lists view
- No mechanism allows User B to discover or access User A's private list
- User B only sees their own lists and lists explicitly shared with the org
- Privacy setting is enforced server-side, not just hidden in the UI

Title: Dashboard Page Loads
Preconditions: User signed in with valid credentials
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront
2. Navigate to /account/dashboard
3. Verify page loads completely
4. Check browser console for JavaScript errors
5. Verify page title and heading display
Expected Result:
- /account/dashboard loads without errors
- No JavaScript console errors present
- Page heading confirms Dashboard context
- All page sections render (no blank areas)
- Network requests complete with 200 status

Title: Recent Orders Widget
Preconditions: User signed in, has at least one previous order
Steps:
1. Sign in and navigate to /account/dashboard
2. Locate the Recent Orders widget/section
3. Verify order entries are displayed
4. Check each entry for order number
5. Check each entry for order date
6. Check each entry for order status
7. Check each entry for order amount
Expected Result:
- Recent Orders section visible on dashboard
- Each order entry shows order number (e.g., ORD-12345)
- Order date displayed per entry
- Order status shown (e.g., Processing, Shipped, Delivered)
- Order amount/total shown per entry
- Orders sorted most-recent first

Title: Monthly Spend Display
Preconditions: User signed in, has orders placed in current calendar month
Steps:
1. Sign in and navigate to /account/dashboard
2. Locate the monthly spending summary widget
3. Note the displayed amount
4. Cross-reference against orders placed this month via Order History
5. Verify totals match
Expected Result:
- Monthly spend widget visible on dashboard
- Amount displayed corresponds to current calendar month
- Value matches sum of current-month orders from Order History
- Currency symbol and formatting correct
- Label clearly indicates the time period (e.g., This Month)

Title: Quick Navigation Links
Preconditions: User signed in, account section accessible
Steps:
1. Sign in and navigate to /account/dashboard
2. Locate quick-navigation links or tiles
3. Click the Orders link
4. Verify navigation to Order History page
5. Return to dashboard
6. Click the Profile link
7. Verify navigation to Profile page
8. Return to dashboard
9. Click the Addresses link
10. Verify navigation to Addresses page
11. Return to dashboard
12. Click the Lists link
13. Verify navigation to Lists page
Expected Result:
- Orders link navigates to /account/orders or Order History
- Profile link navigates to /account/profile
- Addresses link navigates to /account/addresses
- Lists link navigates to /account/lists
- Back-navigation returns to dashboard correctly
- All links resolve without 404 errors

Title: Dashboard After First Login
Preconditions: New user account created, no prior orders or activity
Steps:
1. Register a new user account
2. Sign in for the first time
3. Navigate to /account/dashboard
4. Observe the dashboard empty state
Expected Result:
- Dashboard loads without errors for new user
- Empty state message displayed (no blank or broken sections)
- Helpful prompts or call-to-action shown (e.g., Start shopping, Browse catalog)
- No order widget errors due to missing data
- No spend widget errors due to missing data
- Navigation links still functional

Title: Notification Bell Icon
Preconditions: User signed in, header navigation visible
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront
2. Observe the header navigation bar
3. Locate the notification bell icon
4. Verify badge element on the icon
Expected Result:
- Notification bell icon visible in header navigation
- Badge (numeric counter) displayed on bell icon when notifications exist
- Badge count reflects number of unread notifications
- Icon is visually distinct and tappable

Title: Notification Dropdown Opens
Preconditions: User signed in, at least one notification exists
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront
2. Click the notification bell icon in header
3. Verify dropdown panel opens
4. Inspect notification list order
5. Verify notification entry details
Expected Result:
- Dropdown panel opens on bell click
- Notification list displayed within dropdown
- Notifications ordered most-recent first (newest at top)
- Each notification shows title/description and timestamp
- Dropdown closes when clicking outside or pressing Escape

Title: Mark as Read
Preconditions: User signed in, unread notification exists, badge count visible
Steps:
1. Note the current badge count on the bell icon
2. Open the notification dropdown
3. Click on an unread notification
4. Observe visual state change of the notification
5. Observe the badge count on the bell icon
Expected Result:
- Clicked notification visually changes to read state (e.g., loses bold or highlight)
- Badge count on bell icon decrements by 1
- Notification remains in list but marked as read
- If last unread notification read, badge disappears or shows 0

Title: Empty Notifications
Preconditions: User signed in, account has no notifications
Steps:
1. Sign in with account that has no notifications
2. Click the notification bell icon in header
3. Observe the dropdown content
Expected Result:
- Notification dropdown opens
- No notifications message or equivalent empty-state text displayed
- No broken layout or empty blank area
- Bell icon shows no badge or badge shows 0
- Empty state is styled consistently with design system

Title: Back-in-Stock Page
Preconditions: User signed in, back-in-stock feature enabled
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront
2. Navigate to /account/back-in-stock
3. Verify page loads
4. Observe listed products
Expected Result:
- /account/back-in-stock page loads without errors
- Page heading confirms context (e.g., Back in Stock Alerts)
- If subscriptions exist: product list displays with product name, image, and status
- If no subscriptions: empty state shown with helpful message
- No JavaScript console errors

Title: Subscribe to Alert
Preconditions: User signed in, out-of-stock product exists on PDP
Steps:
1. Navigate to an out-of-stock product detail page
2. Verify Notify When Available or equivalent button is shown
3. Click the button
4. Verify confirmation feedback (toast or inline message)
5. Navigate to /account/back-in-stock
6. Verify the product appears in the subscribed list
Expected Result:
- Notify When Available button visible on OOS product PDP
- Clicking the button shows confirmation (toast, modal, or inline text)
- Product appears in /account/back-in-stock list after subscribing
- Product name and image shown in the list
- Subscription persists after page refresh

Title: Unsubscribe from Alert
Preconditions: User signed in, at least one product subscribed in back-in-stock list
Steps:
1. Navigate to /account/back-in-stock
2. Locate a subscribed product entry
3. Click the remove/unsubscribe action
4. Confirm removal if prompted
5. Verify the product is no longer in the list
6. Navigate back to the product PDP
Expected Result:
- Remove/unsubscribe action available per subscribed product
- Confirmation step shown if applicable
- Product removed from /account/back-in-stock list immediately
- List updates without requiring page refresh
- Product PDP no longer shows active subscription state (if applicable)

Title: Points History Page
Preconditions: User signed in, loyalty/points feature enabled for store
Steps:
1. Sign in to storefront
2. Navigate to /account/points-history
3. Verify page loads
4. Observe points balance display
5. Observe transaction history list
Expected Result:
- /account/points-history page loads without errors
- Current points balance prominently displayed
- Transaction history list shown (date, description, points earned/spent)
- Transactions ordered most-recent first
- No JavaScript console errors

Title: Points After Order
Preconditions: User signed in, loyalty feature enabled, current points balance noted before test
Steps:
1. Note current points balance on /account/points-history
2. Place a new order through checkout
3. Complete order to confirmation page
4. Navigate to /account/points-history
5. Verify new transaction entry
6. Verify points balance has increased
Expected Result:
- New transaction entry appears in points history after order
- Transaction references the order (order number or description)
- Points credited matches expected earn rate for the order
- Points balance updates to reflect new total
- Transaction timestamp reflects order date
